title: Ana Badour
content:
People.ContactEmail abadour@mccarthy.ca
- Osgoode Hall Law School
A leading advisor on financial services regulation and Fintech, Ana brings a practical, business-focused perspective to helping clients launch and scale innovative financial products in a highly regulated environment.
Ana Badour is a Partner in our Business Law Group in Toronto, Head of the Firm’s Financial Institutions Regulatory practice and Co-Leader of the Firm’s Fintech practice. She advises banks, emerging companies and investors across the financial services ecosystem on regulatory compliance, commercial arrangements and strategic initiatives involving innovative financial products and services.
Ranked as a leading lawyer in Chambers Canada for Banking and Finance: Financial Services Regulatory and Fintech Legal, Ana’s practice spans a broad range of financial services matters, including consumer protection, anti-money laundering compliance, payments and credit card programs, and the structuring and launch of new financial services platforms. She also advises on transactions and partnerships involving financial institutions and Fintech entities, supporting clients as they build, partner and scale in a complex regulatory landscape.
Ana brings a rare dual perspective shaped by both private practice and in-house experience. She recently spent time at a Canadian challenger bank, where she worked on the operational and regulatory realities of building and scaling financial products. This experience informs her practical, solutions-oriented advice and her ability to align legal strategy with business objectives.
Active in the financial services and Fintech community, Ana has held leadership roles in industry organizations focused on advocacy and policy development. She currently serves as co-lead of the Government Relations Committee of the Canadian Prepaid Providers Organization and previously co-led the Communications Committee of FDATA. Her work includes engagement on regulatory initiatives affecting innovation in financial services, including consumer-driven banking.
Ana is a frequent speaker and writer on emerging issues in financial services regulation and Fintech.
She was called to the Ontario bar in 2004 and holds an LLB from Osgoode Hall Law School. She also earned an LLM in Banking and Financial Law from Boston University, where she graduated first in her class, and a B. Math (Operations Research) from the University of Waterloo.
